Cancer Type Detailed
Patients have clinical benefit when mRECIST response is different than Progressive Disease.
Clinical stage of cancer.
Non-mutated self-antigens that may be abnormally expressed in tumors. A major subset includes cancer-testis antigens (CTAs), typically restricted to germline tissues but reactivated in cancers.
Arise from aberrant expression of endogenous retroviruses (ERVs), normally silenced genomic elements reactivated in tumors.
Generated when structural variants (e.g., translocations, deletions, inversions) join distant genomic loci, producing novel fusion proteins or transcripts.
Pathway that is being targeted by the ICI treatment.
Drug used for the treatment. Combination therapies, where more than one drug is administered, are represented by the name of both drugs.
What is the target of the ICI drug.
Immune Subtype described in Thorsson et al, 2018 e computed using the Immune Subtype Classifier tool (https://github.com/CRI-iAtlas/ImmuneSubtypeClassifier)
Arise from somatic insertion or deletion mutations that create frameshifts or altered coding sequences, leading to tumor-specific peptides.
Indicates whether the cancer is metastatic or not.
Mutation Count
Which non ICI drugs did a patient get during the study. Combination therapies, where more than one procedure occurs, are represented by the name of both treatments.

Progression-Free Status from Start of treatment
Progressors are defined as patients with mRECIST of Progressive Disease.
Responders are defined as patients with mRECIST of Partial Response or Complete Response, whereas Non-Responders are those with Progressive Disease or Stable Disease.
Response to treatment following modified Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors (mRECIST) guidelines.
Number of Samples Per Patient
The time of sample collection in relation to the ICI treatment time. Most of the datasets collected samples Pre treatment.
Sample Type
Produced by somatic single-nucleotide variants that change amino acids directly or indirectly through downstream coding effects.
Somatic Status
Generated from abnormal splicing events such as intron retention, exon skipping, or mis-splice site usage, producing tumor-specific transcripts.
TIDE prediction of benefit to ICI treatment.
TIDE prediction of response to ICI treatment.
TMB (nonsynonymous)
Derived from proteins encoded by oncogenic viruses (e.g., HPV, EBV, HTLV-1, HCV) that contribute to cancer development in infected tissues.
